Container.Name: A1111-WebUI-Aegisnir
Container.Description:
AUTOMATIC1111 Stable Diffusion WebUI for Unraid.
This template is configured for a locally built image named a1111-webui-aegisnir:latest. Build the image on your Unraid host before creating the container from this template.
## Security Hardening Defaults The template now sets the following security options by default: - --read-only: Makes the container filesystem read-only except for explicitly mounted paths (like /data). - --tmpfs /tmp:rw,noexec,nosuid,size=2g: Provides a safe, writable /tmp. - --security-opt no-new-privileges:true: Prevents processes from gaining new privileges. - --cap-drop=ALL: Drops all Linux capabilities except those required by the base image. - --pids-limit=512: Limits the number of processes to contain runaway spawning.
DATA DIRECTORY WARNING: The path used with --data-dir grows very large very quickly because it can hold models, outputs, extensions, caches, and other working data. I recommend storing it outside of appdata when possible. You can still place it inside appdata if you want the convenience, but it may consume space very quickly and can fill the location you use for Docker-related storage.
The default host path is set to /mnt/user/ai/data/ so the large working set lives outside appdata by default.
Authentication defaults: This container enables the AUTOMATIC1111 login page by default unless you explicitly override auth flags in COMMANDLINE_ARGS. Default WebUI username: admin Default WebUI password placeholder: changeme-now The container will refuse to start until you replace that password or explicitly manage auth via COMMANDLINE_ARGS. You can also mount an auth file and set WEBUI_AUTH_FILE to avoid storing credentials directly in template variables.
Container.Overview:
This template expects a locally built Docker image named a1111-webui-aegisnir:latest. It does not pull from Docker Hub unless you change the Repository field to a published image. The default Extra Parameters include --runtime=nvidia so Docker can expose the NVIDIA runtime inside the container.
This template passes Automatic1111 data storage through --data-dir.
- Models, outputs, extensions, caches, and other working data are stored in the mapped data share instead of the container filesystem. - This allows you to preserve your models and outputs across container updates and manage them from the host more easily. - The data directory can grow very large very quickly, so I recommend storing it outside of appdata when possible. - Appdata works if you prefer it, but be aware of how quickly it can fill your Docker storage area. - If startup fails with a Torch GPU check error, you can temporarily add --skip-torch-cuda-test to COMMANDLINE_ARGS for troubleshooting. - If first-start dependency installation fails, remove /data/venv and retry after rebuilding the image so the bootstrap can recreate a clean environment. - WebUI login is enabled by default unless you explicitly set your own --gradio-auth or --gradio-auth-path in COMMANDLINE_ARGS.

Config.Name: WebUI
Config.Description: Port exposed by the AUTOMATIC1111 WebUI (default: 7860).

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Host path mapped to --data-dir. Default: /mnt/user/ai/data/ (outside appdata).

Config.Name: WebUI Username
Config.Description: Default username for the AUTOMATIC1111 login page. Change this from the default before exposing the container to other users.

Config.Name: WebUI Password
Config.Description: Password for the AUTOMATIC1111 login page. The container will refuse to start until you replace the default placeholder value.

Config.Name: WebUI Auth File
Config.Description: Optional path inside the container to an AUTOMATIC1111 auth file. If set, this takes precedence over WEBUI_USERNAME/WEBUI_PASSWORD unless you explicitly pass --gradio-auth or --gradio-auth-path in COMMANDLINE_ARGS.

Config.Name: API Auth Mode
Config.Description: Controls API authentication. 'mirror-webui' reuses WEBUI_USERNAME/WEBUI_PASSWORD for --api-auth. 'disabled' leaves API auth unset unless you pass your own --api-auth in COMMANDLINE_ARGS. Avoid sharing logs that may include custom auth arguments.

Config.Name: API Auth File Mode
Config.Description: Controls API authentication when WEBUI_AUTH_FILE is set. 'mirror-webui-file' mirrors the auth file into --api-auth. 'disabled' leaves API auth unset unless you pass your own --api-auth in COMMANDLINE_ARGS.

Config.Name: Extra Args
Config.Description: Extra arguments for launch.py. Default includes --data-dir /data, --xformers, and --no-download-sd-model so the container does not silently download a large default checkpoint. Provide your own model under /data/models/Stable-diffusion or override this if you intentionally want automatic download behavior. For GPU errors, try adding --skip-torch-cuda-test temporarily.
